title: cyperus ascofibrillosus
intro: Cyperus ascofibrillosus, also known as the False Umbrella Palm, is a type of sedge that belongs to the Cyperaceae family. It is a tropical plant native to Asia and Africa, and its roots, rhizomes, and leaves are traditionally used in herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of Cyperus ascofibrillosus include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, and its herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
